summary: The IDF has launched a broad offensive in the Bofar ridge and the Nahal Salkhi area of southern Lebanon, involving multiple brigades to destroy Hezbollah infrastructure. Hezbollah rockets fired toward the Zarit and Kiryat Shmona areas were intercepted by Israeli air defenses, and alerts of hostile aircraft have been issued across northern Israel. Iran is discussing a potential US‑Iran deal focused on the Strait of Hormuz while simultaneously rebuilding underground missile stockpiles.
key_points: The IDF began a large‑scale operation in the Bofar ridge and Nahal Salkhi area of southern Lebanon, deploying several ground brigades to destroy infrastructure and eliminate militants (Source: @almogboker78/17223).
Two Hezbollah rockets launched toward the Zarit area on the Lebanon‑Israel border were intercepted, with no injuries reported (Source: @manniefabian/58330).
Two rockets fired by Hezbollah toward Kiryat Shmona were intercepted by the IDF (Source: @manniefabian/58323).
IDF alerts were activated for hostile aircraft incursions in the Zraite, Shomera, and Kfar Giladi areas, with investigations ongoing (Source: @idf_telegram/24012; @idf_telegram/24025).
The IDF announced an evacuation order for residents south of the Zahrani River in Lebanon, warning of forceful action against Hezbollah (Source: @Faytuks_Network/3200).
Iran‑US deal framework discussions include regulation of the Strait of Hormuz and the release of frozen Iranian assets (Source: @presstv/192114).
Iran is rapidly rebuilding its underground missile stockpiles, potentially preparing larger missile launches toward Israel and regional states (Source: @yediotnews25/130186).

sections.executive_summary: The IDF has launched a broad offensive in the Bofar ridge and Nahal Salkhi area of southern Lebanon, deploying multiple brigades to destroy Hezbollah infrastructure. Hezbollah rockets aimed at Israeli border towns were intercepted, and alerts of hostile aircraft have been issued across northern Israel. Iran is negotiating a US‑Iran agreement focused on the Strait of Hormuz while rebuilding underground missile stockpiles.

points: Unofficial details of the Iran‑US deal framework indicate that regulation of the Strait of Hormuz and the release of frozen Iranian assets are central issues under discussion (Source: @presstv/192114).
Iranian media reported an explosion on Qeshm Island in Hormozgan Province, with residents confirming hearing a sound, though the nature of the event remains unknown (Source: @Faytuks_Network/3209).
Iran’s top negotiator Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf stated that no agreement will be accepted until Iran’s rights are secured, underscoring a hard‑line negotiating stance (Source: @Faytuks_Network/3203).

CNN‑cited reporting claims Iran is rapidly rebuilding its underground missile stockpiles, potentially preparing larger missile launches toward Israel and regional states (Source: @yediotnews25/130186).

The IDF confirmed a wide‑scale operation in the Bofar ridge and Nahal Salkhi area of southern Lebanon, involving the Golani, 7th, Givati, Fire Brigades, and a multi‑dimensional unit under Division 36, aimed at destroying infrastructure and eliminating militants (Source: @almogboker78/17223).
Two rockets launched by Hezbollah toward the Zarit area on the Lebanon‑Israel border were intercepted, with no injuries reported (Source: @manniefabian/58330).
Two rockets fired by Hezbollah toward Kiryat Shmona were intercepted by Israeli defenses (Source: @manniefabian/58323).
IDF alerts were activated for suspected hostile aircraft incursions in Zraite, Shomera, and Kfar Giladi, with investigations ongoing (Source: @idf_telegram/24012; @idf_telegram/24025).
The IDF announced an evacuation order for all residents south of the Zahrani River in Lebanon, warning of forceful action against Hezbollah in that area (Source: @Faytuks_Network/3200).
Schools in Kiryat Shmona were canceled due to the worsening security situation (Source: @amitsegal/61515).
The IDF’s operation in the Bofar ridge and Nahal Salkhi area involves multiple ground brigades to expand the forward defense line and eliminate Hezbollah threats (Source: @almogboker78/17223).
Hezbollah launched rockets toward the Zarit border area, which were intercepted by Israeli air defenses (Source: @manniefabian/58330).
Hezbollah also fired rockets toward Kiryat Shmona, intercepted with no reported casualties (Source: @manniefabian/58323).
An IDF evacuation order was issued for residents south of the Zahrani River, indicating preparation for intensified ground operations (Source: @Faytuks_Network/3200).
Lebanese President Joseph Aoun is reported to be leading intensive efforts with the United States to seek a cease‑fire or de‑escalation amid Israeli advances (Source: @New_security8200/200516).
Iran‑US negotiations include discussions on the Strait of Hormuz, a strategic chokepoint affecting regional oil shipments (Source: @presstv/192114).
Reports of an explosion on Qeshm Island in Iran’s Hormozgan Province were noted, though details remain unclear (Source: @Faytuks_Network/3209).

The French Foreign Minister stated that the Strait of Hormuz lies within international waters and must remain open without fees or extortion, reflecting Gulf state concerns over navigation rights (Source: @Faytuks_Network/3206).

key_developments: The IDF launched a broad offensive in the Bofar ridge and Nahal Salkhi area of southern Lebanon, deploying multiple brigades to destroy Hezbollah infrastructure and eliminate militants (Source: @almogboker78/17223).
Two Hezbollah rockets aimed at the Zarit border area were intercepted by Israeli air defenses, with no injuries reported (Source: @manniefabian/58330).
Two rockets fired by Hezbollah toward Kiryat Shmona were intercepted, preventing casualties (Source: @manniefabian/58323).
IDF alerts were activated for suspected hostile aircraft incursions in Zraite, Shomera, and Kfar Giladi, prompting investigations (Source: @idf_telegram/24012).
An evacuation order was issued for all residents south of the Zahrani River in Lebanon, warning of forceful action against Hezbollah (Source: @Faytuks_Network/3200).
Iran‑US deal talks focus on regulating the Strait of Hormuz and releasing frozen Iranian assets (Source: @presstv/192114).
Iran is rapidly rebuilding its underground missile stockpiles, potentially preparing larger missile launches toward Israel and regional states (Source: @yediotnews25/130186).
sections.threat_assessment: Hezbollah continues to launch rockets toward Israeli border towns, but Israeli air defenses have successfully intercepted them, indicating a high‑intensity but contained missile threat. The IDF's ground offensive in southern Lebanon and evacuation orders suggest a preparation for larger conventional engagements. Iran's reconstruction of underground missile facilities and its hard‑line stance in US negotiations raise the risk of a broader regional escalation, especially concerning the strategic Strait of Hormuz.
sections.regional_response: Lebanese leadership is reportedly engaging with the United States to seek a cease‑fire or de‑escalation, reflecting pressure to curb Israeli advances. France has publicly affirmed the freedom of navigation in the Strait of Hormuz, aligning with Gulf state interests. Iran's negotiation posture emphasizes securing national rights, potentially limiting concessions and influencing regional diplomatic dynamics.
sections.intelligence_notes: Monitoring of IDF ground movements in the Bofar and Nahal Salkhi sectors should focus on brigade composition and logistical support to gauge operation depth. Continued interception of Hezbollah rockets indicates effective Israeli air defense but also persistent launch capabilities from Lebanon. Iran's underground missile development warrants satellite and SIGINT surveillance to assess launch readiness and potential export risks.
